title: Scaffolding: project skeleton
description:
Set up the Python project so pip install -e . works and jor --help prints the click command group.

## Acceptance Criteria
- pyproject.toml with: python >=3.11, deps (click, pydantic, pytest as dev dep), [project.scripts] jor = jor.cli:main
- src/jor/__init__.py with __version__
- src/jor/cli.py with click group and 4 stub subcommands (discover, list, convert, open) that print not yet implemented
- tests/conftest.py exists
- pip install -e . succeeds
- jor --help shows the 4 commands
- pytest runs (even if no tests yet)

## Files
- pyproject.toml
- src/jor/__init__.py
- src/jor/cli.py
- tests/conftest.py

title: Impl: CLI commands (discover, list, convert, open)
description:
Implement the jor CLI entry point. All tests and linters must pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- jor discover calls scanner and prints summary (N sessions found)
- jor list prints table of sessions with tool, date, message_count, title
- jor list --tool claude_code filters output
- jor list --query filters by title/first message
- jor convert 1 writes native session file and prints resume command
- jor convert 1 --codex writes Codex format session
- jor open 1 converts AND launches the tool
- CLI exits with code 0 on success, non-zero on error

## Implementation Guidance
- File: src/jor/cli.py
- Use click for CLI framework
- All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/test_cli.py
- Linters pass: uv run ruff check src/

title: Impl: scanner and session index
description:
Implement the scanner and session index. All tests and linters must pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- scanner.scan() calls all registered connectors and collects results
- scanner.scan() skips connectors where detect() returns False
- index.write() writes ~/.jor/index.json with correct structure (version, sessions, last_scan)
- index.read() reads ~/.jor/index.json and returns parsed data
- index.read() on missing file returns empty index
- After scan + write, index contains entries for all found sessions

## Implementation Guidance
- Files: src/jor/discovery/scanner.py, src/jor/discovery/index.py
- All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/test_scanner.py
- Linters pass: uv run ruff check src/

title: Impl: session schema (JorMessage, ToolCall, ToolResult)
description:
Implement Jor session schema Pydantic models. All tests and linters must pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JorMessage validates required fields (id, role, content, timestamp, source_tool)
- JorMessage rejects invalid role values
- ToolCall validates id, name, input fields
- ToolResult validates tool_call_id, content, is_error fields
- JorMessage with tool_calls and tool_result serializes to/from JSON correctly
- JSONL round-trip: write a JorMessage to a line, read it back, same object
- metadata field preserves arbitrary dict data

## Implementation Guidance
- File: src/jor/session/schema.py
- Minimal Pydantic models, no extra logic
- All tests pass: uv run pytest tests/test_schema.py
- Linters pass: uv run ruff check src/

title: Scaffold: project structure and pyproject.toml
description:
Set up the Python project structure for jor CLI.

Create pyproject.toml with uv config, dependencies (pydantic, click), and entry point. Create the src/jor/ directory tree with empty __init__.py files matching the spec. No logic yet — just the scaffolding to make imports and tests work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- pyproject.toml exists with correct metadata, deps, and entry point
- src/jor/ tree matches spec structure
- tests/ directory with conftest.py and fixtures/ subdirectory
- uv run pytest runs without errors (0 tests collected is fine)
- uv run jor --help runs without errors
close_reason: Scaffold structure complete and committed (da9b396). Code verified via inspection; uv verification blocked by permission controls in automated session.

title: Claude Code skill + packaging
description:
Create the Claude Code skill and finalize packaging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- skill/jor.md wraps CLI: discover -> list -> user picks -> convert -> print resume command
- README.md with: what Jor is, install instructions (pip/uvx), usage examples for each command, supported tools
- LICENSE file (MIT)
- pyproject.toml has all metadata for PyPI: description, license, classifiers, urls, author
- pip install . from clean clone works
- jor --help works after install

## Files
- skill/jor.md
- README.md
- LICENSE
- pyproject.toml (update)

title: Impl: CLI commands
description:
Implement CLI commands. All tests and linters must pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- jor discover: calls scanner.scan_all() with registered connectors, prints 'Found N sessions: X claude_code, Y codex', updates index
- jor list: reads index, prints table with columns (ID, Tool, Date, Messages, Title), default limit 20
- jor list --tool codex: filters to codex sessions only
- jor list --query 'auth': filters by title/first message substring match
- jor list --limit 5: limits output rows
- jor list --path /path: filters by workspace path
- jor convert <id>: looks up session by UUID from index, writes to Claude Code format by default, prints file path and resume command
- jor convert <id> --codex: writes to Codex format instead
- jor open <id>: runs convert then launches tool via subprocess
- Errors: 'No sessions found' when index empty, 'Session <id> not found' for invalid UUID

## Implementation Guidance
- Wire click commands to scanner, index, reader, writers
- Use click.echo for output, simple string formatting for table
- subprocess.run for jor open launcher
- Keep it thin: CLI is glue code
- All tests pass: pytest
- Linters pass: ruff check

## Files
- src/jor/cli.py

title: Impl: Codex writer
description:
Implement Codex writer. All tests and linters must pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CodexWriter implements Writer protocol from base.py
- Maps JorMessage back to OpenAI chat format: role, content, tool_calls array
- tool_calls array items have: id, type=function, function={name, arguments}
- Maps role=tool_result back to role=tool with tool_call_id and content
- Writes to target path
- Output file is valid Codex session format (each line is an OpenAI chat message)
- Returns the session ID and file path

## Implementation Guidance
- Writer protocol (base.py) already exists from CC writer impl
- Reverse of the codex connector parsing
- Write JSONL with json.dumps
- All tests pass: pytest
- Linters pass: ruff check

- src/jor/session/writers/codex.py

title: Impl: Claude Code writer
description:
Implement Claude Code writer. All tests and linters must pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Writer protocol defined in base.py: write(messages: list[JorMessage], target_path: Path) -> tuple[str, Path] (session_id, file_path)
- ClaudeCodeWriter implements Writer protocol
- Maps JorMessage role back to Claude Code type field (user, assistant, tool_use, tool_result)
- Reconstructs content blocks: tool calls become content blocks with type=tool_use, tool results become type=tool_result
- Each JSONL line has: type, message (with role, content), timestamp, sessionId
- Writes to target path
- Output file is valid Claude Code session format (parseable by Claude Code connector)
- Returns the session ID and file path

## Implementation Guidance
- Create src/jor/session/writers/base.py with Writer protocol (Protocol class from typing)
- Reverse of the connector parsing logic
- Write JSONL line by line with json.dumps
- Generate session UUID if not provided
- All tests pass: pytest
- Linters pass: ruff check

## Files
- src/jor/session/writers/base.py
- src/jor/session/writers/claude_code.py

title: Impl: session index
description:
Implement session index. All tests and linters must pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- SessionIndex Pydantic model matching spec (version, sessions list, last_scan)
- IndexEntry model with: id, tool, source_id, source_path, title, project, started_at, ended_at, message_count, model, provider, summary
- load_index(path) reads index.json, returns SessionIndex (empty if file missing)
- save_index(index, path) writes SessionIndex to JSON file
- upsert_session(index, entry) adds or updates by id
- Round-trip: save then load produces identical index

## Implementation Guidance
- Pydantic models for IndexEntry and SessionIndex
- Simple JSON file read/write with pathlib
- upsert by matching on id field

title: Impl: session schema
description:
Implement session schema models. All tests and linters must pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JorMessage Pydantic model with fields: id, role (user|assistant|system|tool_result), content, tool_calls, tool_result, files, model, provider, timestamp, metadata, source_tool, source_id
- ToolCall model with: id, name, input (dict)
- ToolResult model with: tool_call_id, content, is_error
- Optional fields are truly optional (None default)
- Serialize a JorMessage to JSON string (one JSONL line)
- Deserialize a JSON string back to JorMessage losslessly
- Invalid role values are rejected by validation
- Round-trip: message -> json line -> message produces identical object

## Implementation Guidance
- Use Pydantic BaseModel
- Use Literal for role validation
- Minimal code to make tests pass
- All tests pass: pytest
- Linters pass: ruff check

## Files
- src/jor/session/schema.py
close_reason: Implemented src/jor/session/schema.py with ToolCall, ToolResult, and JorMessage Pydantic models. Role validated via Literal type. All optional fields default None. Supports model_dump_json() and model_validate_json() for JSONL round-trips.

title: Claude Code skill: jor.md
